title: Server Variables for Pre- and Post-Requests
breadcrumbs: Server Variables for Pre- and Post-Requests
text: Server Variables for Pre- and Post-Requests The functions soap_pre_request() and soap_post_request() take a list of server variables as the first parameter. The *vars parameter is a null-terminated string in the UTF-8 character set. It is an unnamed Uniface list that contains a named list (SERVERVARIABLES) of the server variables supported by the Uniface Web Request Dispatcher (WRD). This named list contains information gathered in the Web server about the request. For more information about these server variables, see $webinfo . The format of the Uniface list is: ServerVariable = { Value1 }{{ LevelIndicator ...} Separator Var2 = { Value2 }}{{ LevelIndicator ...} Separator Var3 = { Value3 }}... where: ServerVariable —name of a variable Value1 —value of ServerVariable . Any Value can also be a Uniface list, as indicated by the presence of a LevelIndicator byte directly in front of the Separator byte. The value of an element in such an embedded list can also be a list, in which case two LevelIndicator bytes precede the Separator byte. The number of consecutive LevelIndicator bytes is the embedding level in the list. Uniface reserves the right to extend the information provided in this parameter at some future date, including adding other Uniface sublists, which is why this parameter is not a single-level Uniface list, but a Uniface list embedded within a Uniface list. LevelIndicator —level indicator byte of value 0x15 Separator —separator byte of value 0x1b { } —optional ... —preceding element can be repeated For example, the following is an unnamed Uniface list that contains the named list SERVERVARIABLES of the server variables: SERVERVARIABLES= REMOTE_USER= LevelIndicator Separator REMOTE_HOST=127.0.0.1 LevelIndicator Separator REMOTE_ADDR=127.0.0.1 LevelIndicator Separator AUTH_TYPE= LevelIndicator Separator PATH_INFO=/KURB112S LevelIndicator Separator PATH_TRANSLATED=D:/uniface/webapps/uniface/KURB112S LevelIndicator Separator SERVER_NAME=127.0.0.1 LevelIndicator Separator SERVER_PORT=80 LevelIndicator Separator SERVER_PROTOCOL=HTTP/1.0 LevelIndicator Separator CONTENT_TYPE=text/xml;charset="UTF-8" LevelIndicator Separator CONTENT_LENGTH=659 LevelIndicator Separator REQUEST_METHOD=POST LevelIndicator Separator REQUEST_URI=/uniface/services/KURB112S LevelIndicator Separator SERVLET_PATH=/services LevelIndicator Separator QUERY_STRING= Copy Note that the CONTENT_TYPE=text/xml;charset="UTF-8" variable contains and equals (=) sign. This format with two equal signs would normally indicate the name and start of a named Uniface list. However, because the level has not changed (there is only one LevelIndicator ), it must be interpreted as an equal sign in the data. If at some future date we needed to append another list of information called NEWLIST, the above string might be extended as follows: Separator NEWLIST=NAME1=VALUE1 LevelIndicator Separator NAME2=VALUE2 <Lev> <Sep>…. Related Links soap_pre_request Receives the SOAP envelope just before Uniface would normally process it, and returns either an amended SOAP envelope that Uniface processes, or returns an error message that will be encapsulated in a SOAP Fault element and sent as a reply to the Web server. soap_post_request Receives the SOAP envelope containing the reply that Uniface produced and returns the SOAP envelope to be sent as a reply to the Web server.
